# Test-Quality Review: `lib/features/ai/state/` + `test/features/ai/state/`

**Scope:** All implementation files under `lib/features/ai/state/` and their counterpart test files under `test/features/ai/state/`.
**Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis)**

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` — 2470 lines for a single test file far exceeds the ~500-line preferred size. The file covers four logically separate provider families that can be mirror-split to match the logical seams in the implementation:
  - `unified_ai_controller_test.dart` — `UnifiedAiState` equality + `UnifiedAiController` (~lines 167–597)
  - `available_skills_for_entity_provider_test.dart` — `availableSkillsForEntityProvider` + `hasAvailableSkillsProvider` (~lines 599–1511)
  - `trigger_skill_provider_test.dart` — `triggerSkillProvider` (~lines 1513–2469)
  - `trigger_new_inference_provider_test.dart` — `triggerNewInferenceProvider` (~lines 686–947)
  The shared `_TestBench` / helper setup should be extracted into a file-local helper to stay DRY across the split files.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/settings/inference_model_form_controller_dirty_state_test.dart` (308 lines) and `test/features/ai/state/settings/inference_provider_form_controller_dirty_state_test.dart` (323 lines) both violate the one-test-file-per-source-file rule. Their tests must be merged back into their respective primary test files (`inference_model_form_controller_test.dart` and `inference_provider_form_controller_test.dart`).

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller.dart` — 635 lines. Three conceptually separate provider families live here: `UnifiedAiController` + `triggerNewInferenceProvider` (controller wiring), `availableSkillsForEntityProvider` + `hasAvailableSkillsProvider` (entity capability queries), and `triggerSkillProvider` (skill dispatch). Splitting into `unified_ai_controller.dart`, `available_skills_providers.dart`, and `trigger_skill_provider.dart` would bring each file under ~250 lines.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/embedding_backfill_controller_test.dart` — 1268 lines. The `backfillAgentReports` group (lines ~780–1165) is independently testable and could be extracted to `embedding_backfill_agent_reports_test.dart`, but only after resolving the one-file-per-source rule: given this rule the preferred approach would be a nested group rather than a new file. However the current file at 1268 lines is large enough to warrant investigating a logical reorganization within the single file using tighter grouping.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/ai/state/linked_entity_inference_test.dart` — 710 lines covering behavior already partially exercised in `unified_ai_controller_test.dart`. The overlap (linked-entity status propagation) should be audited for redundancy; unique scenarios should be folded into the unified controller test after the split described above.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` — `MockUnifiedAiInferenceRepository` is declared inline at line 37–38. The same class is also declared inline in `test/features/ai/state/linked_entity_inference_test.dart` (line 16–17). Neither file uses the centralized `test/mocks/mocks.dart`. `MockUnifiedAiInferenceRepository` should be added to `test/mocks/mocks.dart` and the inline declarations removed from both files.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` — 18 manual `getIt.isRegistered` / `getIt.unregister` / `getIt.registerSingleton` calls in `setUp` (lines 96–124). This is precisely the anti-pattern that `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()` from `test/widget_test_utils.dart` is designed to replace.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` — `AiConfigPrompt` is constructed verbatim ~8 times across tests (lines 211, 302, 427, 509, 688, 754, 853, 862 and more). Each construction is nearly identical (same `systemMessage`, `userMessage`, `defaultModelId`, `modelIds`, `createdAt`, `useReasoning`, `requiredInputData`) with only `id` and `aiResponseType` varying. A shared helper `_makePromptConfig({required String id, required AiResponseType responseType})` would eliminate the copy-paste.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/profile_automation_providers_test.dart` — Lines 42–63: three tests assert only `isA<ProfileResolver>()`, `isA<ProfileAutomationResolver>()`, and `isA<ProfileAutomationService>()`. These are pure constructor smoke tests — they prove the provider wires up without crashing but assert no behavior. Per AGENTS.md rule: "No constructor smoke tests." The tests should be replaced with or supplemented by tests that verify the resolver/service actually produces correct results (e.g., that `profileResolverProvider` returns a resolver that correctly looks up a profile by ID from a stubbed repository).

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/embedding_backfill_controller_test.dart` — Line 697: `await Future<void>.delayed(Duration.zero)` inside a test that checks `state().isRunning`. This is a real-time delay in a non-I/O test and violates the fake-time policy. The test relies on observing that `isRunning` is set to `true` between the first and second call; this should be driven by `async.flushMicrotasks()` inside `fakeAsync`, or by restructuring the test to observe the state transition deterministically rather than relying on a scheduler yield.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/linked_entity_inference_test.dart` — Lines 35–57: `setUp` manually registers `DomainLogger` via `getIt.isRegistered` / `getIt.unregister` / `getIt.registerSingleton`. Same pattern as `unified_ai_controller_test.dart`. Should use `setUpTestGetIt()`.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` — Lines 300–422: the test `'successfully runs inference and updates state'` wraps an already-async scenario inside `fakeAsync` and then drives `async.elapse(Duration(milliseconds: 10))` twice to match two `Future<void>.delayed(const Duration(milliseconds: 10))` calls embedded in the mock answer (lines 354, 356). The `Duration(milliseconds: 10)` delays inside the mock answer are real async delays placed inside `fakeAsync` — this is the pattern described in test/README.md under "Replacing Duration.zero Yields". They should be replaced with synchronous callbacks, or the mock answer should complete immediately so the `elapse` calls are unnecessary.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/consts_test.dart` — Lines 79–104: `icon` test and lines 45–77 `localizedName` test both iterate all 7 enum values with separate `expect` lines, giving 7 copy-paste expect calls per test. These can be parameterized using a `final cases = <AiResponseType, IconData>{...}` map iterated in a loop, reducing each test body to ~5 lines.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` — `FakeEntryControllerNull` (lines 41–43) is defined inline. If this pattern is needed in other test files it should be added to central fakes; if unique to this file it should remain but be documented.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/ai/state/linked_entity_inference_test.dart` — Line 94: `tearDown` only unregisters `DomainLogger` but `setUp` also registers it. If the test suite is expanded the teardown may become incomplete. Should mirror the full registration/unregistration via `setUpTestGetIt()`.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller.dart` — `availableSkillsForEntityProvider` contains a pure filter function with discrete input dimensions: `hasTaskContext: bool`, `entity type: {JournalEntry, JournalAudio, Task, JournalImage, Other}`, `skill.contextPolicy: ContextPolicy enum`, `skill.requiredInputModalities: List<Modality>`, and `skill.skillType: SkillType`. The combinatorial space (5 entity types × 4 context policies × 5 modalities × 5 skill types) is large enough that the existing hand-written example tests (lines 949–1511) still leave gaps (e.g., a skill requiring both audio AND text modalities, or a skill with `ContextPolicy.dictionaryOnly`). A Glados property: "for any generated skill/entity pair, `availableSkillsForEntityProvider` returns the skill iff all modality, contextPolicy, and supportedType predicates are simultaneously satisfied" would be a genuine improvement. The logic is pure (given mocked entry state) and not UI or I/O bound. Tag: `glados`.

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/embedding_backfill_controller.dart` — `_processEntities` has non-trivial arithmetic: `progress = processed / total`, counts for `embedded`, `failed`, `processed`. A Glados property over randomly generated sequences of `didEmbed: bool` and `didThrow: bool` per entity could verify that `processed == embedded + failed + (skipped by cancel)`, `progress == processed / total`, and `embedded + failed <= processed` hold for any input sequence. This is pure math over counters, ideal for property testing.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/features/ai/state/consts.dart` — `SkillTypeToResponseType.toResponseType` already has a Glados test in `consts_test.dart` — well covered. No gap here.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/features/ai/state/consts.dart` — `isLegacyType` could have a Glados property: "for any `AiResponseType` value, `isLegacyType` equals `this == taskSummary || this == checklistUpdates || this == imageGeneration`". However this is trivially verified by the existing static tests; only worth adding Glados if a regression in the formula becomes a concern.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `lib/features/ai/state/consts.dart` line 112–117: `isLegacyType` includes `AiResponseType.imageGeneration`, but `imageGeneration` is NOT annotated `@Deprecated` in the enum (lines 60–62). It is a current, active skill type. This is likely a logic bug — `imageGeneration` should not be classified as a legacy type. No test covers `AiResponseType.imageGeneration.isLegacyType`; the existing `isPromptGenerationType` tests do not touch `isLegacyType`. A test asserting `AiResponseType.imageGeneration.isLegacyType == false` would surface this discrepancy.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `lib/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller.dart` — `_performInference` error path, lines 329–344: the second `ref.read(aiConfigByIdProvider(promptId).future)` inside the catch block (to set error status) has its own try/catch that silently swallows failures. There is no test for the case where `promptId` is invalid/not found (config returns `null` or throws) specifically during the error handler. The existing error test (line 507) uses a valid promptId. A test where `promptId` resolves to `null` would exercise line 246's null branch and verify the `Exception('Invalid prompt configuration')` is correctly thrown and caught.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `lib/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller.dart` — `triggerSkillProvider` error handler (lines 596–603): the entire provider body is wrapped in try/catch that routes to `loggingService.error`. No test exercises a `StateError` being thrown from inside `runner.runTranscription/runImageAnalysis/runPromptGeneration`. The only error test is for `imageGeneration` with null `linkedTaskId` (line 2359). Add coverage for runner methods throwing during execution.

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/embedding_backfill_controller.dart` — `_EmbedResult` class (lines 353–361) is constructed inside `_processEntities` but its return value is never used by either `backfillCategories` or `backfillAgentReports`. This is dead code. The class and its construction should be removed, or the backfill methods should be updated to use the returned counts (which would enable `backfillCategories` to accumulate `failed` counts from multiple categories correctly — currently each category resets the offset only if processed via the offsets, but the returned result is discarded).

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/embedding_backfill_controller.dart` — `backfillCategories` (line 247): the `processedOffset`, `embeddedOffset`, `failedOffset` parameters of `_processEntities` are always passed as 0 even when iterating multiple categories. This means if 2 categories are processed, the second category's `_processEntities` call starts with `processed=0`, overwriting the state from the first call. The test at line 287 (`processes entities across multiple categories`) passes because it uses a single call that aggregates all IDs before passing them to `_processEntities` — the multi-category test actually deduplicates into one list so only one `_processEntities` call is made. The offset parameters of `_processEntities` are thus unused dead parameters. No test exercises them being non-zero.

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/settings/inference_model_form_controller.dart` line 193: `updatedAt: DateTime.now()` — non-deterministic. There is no test for the timestamp set on update; if a test ever needs to assert the updatedAt value it will be flaky. The controller should accept a clock dependency (or a `DateTime Function()` parameter defaulting to `DateTime.now`) for testability.

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/features/ai/state/profile_automation_providers.dart` — `categoryProfileLookup` (lines 32–37): the closure calls `journalDb.getCategoryById(categoryId)` and reads `category?.defaultProfileId`. No test currently exercises the path where `getCategoryById` returns a non-null category with a `defaultProfileId`. The `profile_automation_providers_test.dart` tests for task profile lookups but the category lookup path is only indirectly covered via `triggerSkillProvider` tests with a real category ID.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/features/ai/state/ai_config_initialization.dart` — The `ModelPrepopulationService.backfillNewModels()` error path logs and continues; `ai_config_initialization_test.dart` line 122 covers this. Clean.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/features/ai/state/inference_status_controller.dart` — `InferenceRunningController` returns `true` if any of the supplied `responseTypes` is `running`. The existing Glados test covers the `setStatus` → read path but there is no test for `InferenceRunningController` with an empty `responseTypes` set (should always return `false`) or with a set that includes a non-running type alongside a running type. These edge cases are not exercised.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` — Lines 341–362: the mock answer for `runInference` contains two `await Future<void>.delayed(const Duration(milliseconds: 10))` calls (lines 354, 356), and similarly in the linked-entity test at lines 823 and 921. These are real async delays embedded inside `fakeAsync`-driven tests, which defeats the purpose of `fakeAsync` and introduces real wall-clock time into the test run. All occurrences should be removed: the mock callbacks should fire synchronously, and the `async.elapse(const Duration(milliseconds: 10))` calls (lines 387–389) can remain to advance fake time if the production code uses timers, but the mock itself must not block on real time. **Impact: ~4 tests × 20ms real delay = ~80ms saved per run, plus removal of potential flakiness.**

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/ai/state/embedding_backfill_controller_test.dart` line 697: `await Future<void>.delayed(Duration.zero)` used to let `isRunning` propagate. Replace with `await Future<void>.value()` which also yields the event loop but is semantically clearer, or restructure the test to observe state via a listener rather than relying on a scheduler yield. **Impact: minor per-run savings but eliminates a policy violation.**

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` — Each of the ~56 tests that requires a `ProviderContainer` with overrides constructs its container fresh. The outer `container` variable is replaced mid-test in several tests (e.g., line 526), leaving the original container to be disposed in tearDown. This per-test container creation is correct but the pattern of re-assigning the shared `container` variable mid-test (rather than using `testContainer` + `containersToDispose`) is inconsistent and error-prone. Standardizing on the `testContainer + containersToDispose` pattern throughout eliminates teardown uncertainty.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/active_inference_controller_test.dart` — Glados uses `numRuns: 180` with a list length range 0–55 (line 251). Each run generates up to 55 operations over 2 entity slots × 5 response types = 10 independent provider keys. This is CPU-intensive. Reducing to `numRuns: 120` and max list length 40 would still be thorough and cut ~33% of CI Glados time for this test.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/state/inference_status_controller_test.dart` — Glados uses `numRuns: 180` with list length 0–55 (inferred from similar pattern). Same recommendation: 120 runs / max 40 operations.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/ai/state/unified_ai_controller_test.dart` — `setUpAll` registers fallback values that include constructing real `JournalEntry` and `ProviderContainer` instances. These are cheap but if the test file is split as recommended, each split file re-runs `setUpAll`. Centralizing fallback registration in `test/helpers/fallbacks.dart` (which is the prescribed pattern via `registerAllFallbackValues()`) and calling that from `setUpAll` would keep consistency.

---

## Summary

- 1 oversized impl file (`unified_ai_controller.dart`, 635 lines — 3 provider families in one file)
- 3 oversized test files (`unified_ai_controller_test.dart` 2470, `embedding_backfill_controller_test.dart` 1268, `inference_provider_form_controller_test.dart` 1303) plus 2 test-splitting violations (`*_dirty_state_test.dart`)
- Weak tests: 3 constructor smoke-tests in `profile_automation_providers_test.dart`; inline mock duplication across 2 files; 18 manual GetIt registrations violating `setUpTestGetIt()` rule; repeated `AiConfigPrompt` construction (~8 copies)
- 2 genuine Glados candidates: `availableSkillsForEntityProvider` pure filter logic; `_processEntities` counter arithmetic
- 3 test-speed wins: real `Future.delayed` delays inside `fakeAsync` tests (~80ms/run); `Duration.zero` yield violation; oversized Glados `numRuns` (180→120) in 2 files
- Biggest single opportunity: split `unified_ai_controller_test.dart` (2470 lines) into ~4 focused files while extracting the shared mock and `AiConfigPrompt` factory — this alone would halve average test-file review cost and remove the two most significant quality violations (inline mock + GetIt boilerplate) in the process
